title: macaranga dallachyi
intro: Macaranga dallachyi, also known as 'Dahonan' in some regions, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly found in the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. Its health benefits include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antidote for snake bites. Traditionally, Macaranga dallachyi is prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and its roots and leaves are also used in traditional medicine, often mixed with other herbs to create a treatment for various ailments.
